evals.name: simple-typo-detection
evals.prompt: I have an open PR and received a review comment saying 'The identifer field should be validated before use'. The code nearby has an 'identifier' field. Analyze this review and tell me if it's actionable.
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect that 'identifer' is a typo for 'identifier' using Levenshtein distance (distance 1), recognize this as actionable feedback, and provide guidance on how to validate the field.

assertions.name: typo_detected
assertions.description: Skill correctly identifies 'identifer' as a typo for 'identifier' with Levenshtein distance detection

assertions.name: actionable_classification
assertions.description: Feedback is correctly classified as actionable (improves code quality)

evals.name: out-of-scope-feedback
evals.prompt: A reviewer on my small PR that fixes a button color says 'You should refactor the entire component system to use a design tokens library'. Should I address this feedback?
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this as out-of-scope feedback (architectural change on a small cosmetic PR) and recommend drafting a clarification reply rather than applying the fix.

assertions.name: scope_evaluation
assertions.description: Skill correctly identifies the feedback as out-of-scope

assertions.name: problematic_classification
assertions.description: Feedback is correctly classified as problematic, not actionable

evals.name: dip-violation-detection
evals.prompt: A reviewer on my PR suggests that instead of hardcoding `new ConsoleLogger()` in the constructor, I should inject it as a dependency. The current code has: `this.logger = new ConsoleLogger()`. Should I apply this change? It seems like it would just add an extra parameter.
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this follows Dependency Inversion Principle better, explain why (loose coupling, testability), acknowledge the tradeoff (adds constructor parameter), and recommend the change while explaining the real-world benefits.

assertions.name: dip_violation_detected
assertions.description: Skill identifies hardcoded dependency as violating Dependency Inversion Principle

assertions.name: improvement_suggested
assertions.description: Suggestion includes dependency injection pattern as the refactored approach

assertions.name: tradeoff_acknowledged
assertions.description: Response acknowledges added complexity vs. improved testability and flexibility

evals.name: multiple-typos
evals.prompt: A reviewer writes: 'In the getUserName function, change the parametr to usr_object and update the identifer reference'. There are typos in 'parametr' (should be 'parameter') and 'identifer' (should be 'identifier'). How should I interpret this feedback?
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect multiple typos using Levenshtein distance and help understand the reviewer's true intent despite the spelling errors.

assertions.name: multiple_typos_detected
assertions.description: Multiple typos are detected: parametr, identifer

assertions.name: intent_inference
assertions.description: Skill infers the reviewer likely meant 'parameter' and 'identifier' based on Levenshtein distance

evals.name: vague-feedback-handling
evals.prompt: A reviewer just says 'This doesn't seem right' without any specific suggestions. How should I handle this?
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this as vague, unhelpful feedback and recommend drafting a professional, empathetic question asking for more specific feedback.

assertions.name: vague_feedback_detected
assertions.description: Skill identifies feedback as vague and uninformative

assertions.name: empathetic_reply_drafted
assertions.description: Skill drafts a professional clarification question
